breaking.title: At least 14 children killed in Pakistan’s Lahore when tutoring centre roof collapses
breaking.summary: Preliminary reports showed the tutoring centre was unregistered and operating inside a privately owned ‌residential building under a dilapidated ​roof

breaking.title: ‘Microphone theory’: Candace Owens revives controversial claim in Charlie Kirk assassination case
breaking.summary: Charlie Kirk was killed during a TPUSA event in Utah in September 2025, and prosecutors accuse Tyler James Robinson of carrying out a politically motivated assassination. While investigators say forensic evidence strongly supports the lone-gunman case, Candace Owens has continued raising doubts and promoting the “exploding microphone” theory. Her claims have fueled debate online as Robinson’s high-profile case heads toward trial.

breaking.title: 'Ready for war': Iran draws red line for US, says no final talks until MoU terms are met
breaking.summary: Iran's Parliament Speaker has issued a stark warning of war if the US fails to adhere to a memorandum of understanding, emphasizing that negotiations will halt until Washington fulfills its commitments. Tehran views recent Persian Gulf incidents as violations, vowing a strong reaction. Discussions are currently focused on implementing the MoU, including the lifting of a naval blockade and the release of frozen assets, with technical talks planned in Doha.

breaking.title: Seven killed, over 15 injured as bus rams into truck on Delhi-Mumbai Expressway
breaking.summary: The accident took place near Dhanawda under the Kolwa police station area.

breaking.title: Firefighters struggle to contain deadly Greek wildfire
breaking.summary: More than 100 firefighters are fighting a fire near Thessaloniki which has claimed at least two lives.

breaking.title: Three-year-old rescued and taken to hospital six days after Venezuela quake
breaking.summary: Video footage shows rescuers cheering as the child is pulled from rubble in La Guaira state.

breaking.title: West Asia war LIVE: Iran was unable to export 'single barrel of oil' during U.S. blockade
breaking.summary: We are pursuing dialogue, but if it is not implemented, we are also prepared for war and will respond accordingly, said Iran's Chief negotiator, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf.

breaking.title: Oil ticks higher as Iran's refusal to meet US envoys dims ceasefire hopes
breaking.summary: Oil prices saw an uptick as Iran's refusal to meet U.S. envoys heightened tensions, impacting the fragile ceasefire. Despite recent declines following conflict de-escalation, analysts are revising forecasts. Meanwhile, U.S. crude and gasoline inventories dropped last week, with markets awaiting official government data.  Tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is reportedly recovering, easing supply disruption fears.
